extracted_json.title: Step 4: Determining Company Position / Competitive Strategy in the I/M Landscape

blocks.text: Determine or decide on the company's position or competitive strategy to compete successfully in the I/M landscape

items.text: (i) unique product, as perceived by the customers, differentiated by attributes such as quality (additional features), reliability, user-interface, ... .
Market dimension (or Target market for the product), which has 2 extremes
(i) a product for the entire market
(ii) a product for a particular (or niche) segment of the market.

extracted_json.summary_topic_line: Step 4 of competitive strategy: positioning a company on a 2D product/market grid in the I/M landscape.
extracted_json.summary_gist: These notes describe Step 4 of a process for deciding a company's competitive strategy to compete in the I/M landscape. The method uses a 2-dimensional grid. The product dimension ranges between two extremes: a unique product (differentiated by attributes like quality, reliability, and user-interface) and a lowest-cost product. The market dimension (target market) also ranges between two extremes: a product for the entire market versus a product for a particular or niche market segment.

transcription:
Step 4 : Determine or decide on the
            company's position or competitive
            strategy to compete successfully
            in the I/M landscape

Process :   Create a 2-dimensional grid
            - Product dimension, which has
              2 extremes
              (i) unique product, as perceived
                  by the customers, differentiated
                  by attributes such as
                  quality (additional features),
                  reliability, user-interface, ... .
              (ii)  lowest cost product

            - Market dimension (or Target
              market for the product), which
              has 2 extremes
              (i) a product for the entire market
              (ii) a product for a particular
                   (or niche) segment of the
                   market.
